title: A Fyre Festival Documentary Arms Race, and Bing Liu on Creating ‘Minding the Gap’ | Interview (Ep. 118)
format: Two documentaries about the same failed music festival land four days apart, then Bing Liu on making Minding the Gap.

segments.heading: The Fyre Festival Documentary Arms Race
segments.note: Netflix vs. Hulu, with Rob Harvilla

films.title: Fyre: The Greatest Party That Never Happened
films.note: Chris Smith's Netflix doc. Fennessey and Harvilla both rate it the better film: it lays out the logistics forensically and keeps the blame on Billy McFarland. Co-produced with Vice; Jerry Media, the festival's own marketers, also produced.

films.title: Fyre Fraud
films.note: Hulu's surprise Monday drop, co-produced with Billboard and Mic. It lands the paid McFarland interview but leans on Simpsons/Family Guy clips and a millennials-did-it-to-themselves angle Harvilla found corny and exculpatory.

films.title: Minding the Gap
films.note: Liu's Rockford, Illinois portrait of three young skateboarders and the abuse in their homes. Fennessey's favorite film of 2018. Started as a nationwide project on domestic violence before Liu narrowed it to Keir, Zach, and himself, adding archival footage once he became a character.

films.title: Hoop Dreams
films.note: Liu first saw it, and Steve James's other work, after joining a Kartemquin fellowship. That's when he turned toward character-driven verite.

films.title: Waking Life
films.note: His first teenage film was a takeoff of it, an improvised philosophical conversation among Rockford's freaks and geeks.

films.title: Shoplifters
films.note: The last great thing he'd seen: warm, loving, a family he'd want to live with.

films: Panic: The Untold Story of the 2008 Financial Crisis
